data.title: Magnite shares soar after bagging two-year extension from Disney for ad services
Swedbank AB EPS of SEK3.64
data.description:

data.title: Assa Abloy EPS of SEK 3.63, revenue of SEK 37.42B
data.description:
• Assa Abloy press release (OTCPK:ASAZF): Q3 EPS of SEK 3.63.
  • Revenue of SEK 37.42B
  • It is estimated that completed acquisitions and divestments, on a rolling 12-month basis as of September 30, 2024, will have an effect of 5% on sales in thefourth quarter of 2024 versus the same period last year, while the effect on theoperating margin is estimated to be neutral in the fourth quarter of 2024.

data.title: Calidi Biotherapeutics stock tumbles on $2M securities offering
data.description:
• Calidi Biotherapeutics (NYSE:CLDI) shares fell over 10% Wednesday morning after the company priced an offering of 2.05M common shares at $1.00 per share.

  • The clinical-stage biotechnology company also entered into a concurrent private placement to issue series E warrants to purchase up to 2.05M common shares and series F warrants to purchase up to 2.05M common shares.

  • The series E warrants have an exercise price of $1.13 per share, while the series F warrants are exercisable at $1.13 per share.

  • The gross proceeds from the offerings are expected to be approximately $2M.

  • Calidi (CLDI) intends to use the net proceeds for working capital, general corporate purposes and pre-clinical and clinical trials.

  • Shares of the biotech firm were down more than 10% in premarket trading on Wednesday.

data.title: Atlas Copco GAAP EPS of SEK 1.47, revenue of SEK 43.1B
data.description:
• Atlas Copco press release (OTCPK:ATLKY): Q3 GAAP EPS of SEK 1.47  
  • Revenue of SEK 43.1B (-3.1% Y/Y)
  • Orders received reached 42,080 MSEK
  • Operating profit reached 9,337 MSEK, corresponding to a margin of 21.7%
  • Operating cash flow at 7,545 MSEK
  • Near-term outlookAtlas Copco Group expects that the customer activity will weaken somewhat.

data.title: Global Payments to offer merchants new checkout solutions with PayPal's Fastlane
data.description:
Global Payments (NYSE:GPN) is enhancing its checkout offerings by integrating PayPal Holdings' (NASDAQ:PYPL) Fastlane payment method, thereby enhancing consumers' shopping experience and driving increased conversion rates for U.S. businesses leveraging the platform, PayPal said on Wednesday.

The two companies have been working together for more than 15 years, with Global Payments' (GPN) serving as a top acquirer of PayPal's (PYPL) branded checkout solutions across Europe, the U.K., Canada, and beyond.

Fastlane allows users to complete purchases in as little as one click. The solution can help deliver nearly 50% higher checkout conversion rates compared to traditional guest checkout flows for consumers paying with a card, while reducing checkout time by more than 28%, PayPal said, citing internal data recorded from Aug. 2 to Sept. 30.

In premarket trading, (GPN) edged up 0.7%, while (PYPL) slipped 0.3%.

data.title: Frontier Comm holder Glendon Capital calls for vote on Verizon deal to be delayed
data.description:
Frontier Communications (NASDAQ:FYBR) holder Glendon Capital Management is calling for the special meeting on the firm's planned sale to Verizon (VZ) to be delayed, as he believes the deal undervalues the firm.

Glendon plans to vote against the deal and believes that Frontier's enterprise value is at least $26 billion, 30% higher than the current $20 billion value ascribed from the Verizon deal, the fund wrote in a letter to Frontier's board on Wednesday.

"With the current voting deadline on November 13, shareholders are being deprived of this critical opportunity to understand the standalone case for the Company," Glendon Partner Holly Kim Olson wrote in the letter. "We find these decisions to be disenfranchising to shareholders."

The Glendon comments come after Reuters reported last month that Glendon, Frontier's second-largest shareholder with a 10% stake, plans to vote against its sale to Verizon (VZ). Glendon plans to oppose the sale, as it believes Verizon's $38.50 a share offer for Frontier is too low.

"Absent a price that appropriately reflects the value of Frontier’s assets and the Company’s earnings trajectory—and an extension of the record date and date of the special meeting of stockholders— we intend to vote our shares against" the Verizon (VZ) deal, Glendon's Olson added in the letter.

data.title: WidePoint secures $15.2M in new and renewal contracts in Q3
data.description:
• WidePoint Corporation (NYSE:WYY) on Wednesday said the company was awarded about $15.2M in new contracts and renewals during Q3 2024.
  • Of the $15.2M in contract awards, the company received $12M in government contracts and the remaining $3.2M in commercial contracts.
  • "Q3 2024 confirms that each of WidePoint's solution lines is gaining market momentum. We are excited to see this momentum continuing through Q4 2024 and into 2025," said Jin Kang, CEO, WidePoint.
  • WYY +2.68% premarket to $3.82.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SatixFy signs new agreements worth $10M
data.description:
• SatixFy Communications (NYSE:SATX) on Wednesday said it has signed new agreements worth $5 million, alongside an immediate prepayment of a $5 million associated with an amendment to an existing contract with MDA Space (MDA:CA).
  • Under an amendment to the original agreement, signed in October 2023, a total of $3 million was added to the scope of the original agreement, alongside an immediate prepayment of $5 million that was previously announced but not yet paid.
  • Additionally, an Authorization to Proceed agreement was signed between SatixFy and MDA Space (MDA:CA), which is structured around two milestone payments totaling $2 million in the aggregate and setting out the terms for future purchase orders.
  • SATX +6.39% premarket to $0.75.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: McCormick establishes growth plans for next 5 years
data.description:
McCormick & Company (NYSE:MKC) is expected to build on Tuesday’s gains as the company reaffirmed its expectations for 2024 and set upbeat growth expectations for the next five years.

“We have robust plans that leverage the demand for flavor and the strength of our brands. We are increasing our investments in the core categories that drive the greatest value for McCormick..and I am confident we have the right leadership team in place and engaged employees globally to deliver on our long-term objectives,” McCormick CEO Brendan Foley said.

The company expects 2024 sales growth to be down 1% to up 1% with minimal impact from currency fluctuations, with a profit per share of $2.85 to $2.90, up from a previous forecast of $2.76 to $2.81, versus the consensus estimate of $2.92 (also revised from $2.87 initially). McCormick (MCK) now expects to achieve the mid to high-end of its projected net sales growth range and the high-end of its adjusted EPS.

Over the next five years until 2028, the company expects net sales to grow 4% to 6%, operating income to increase by 7% to 9% and earnings to improve by 9% to 11%, with $8B in annual net sales by 2028.

Since the beginning of the year, McCormick shares have underperformed the S&P 500 as shifting discretionary spending has adversely impacted names in the sector like McCormick (MKC), Darling Ingredients (DAR), and J.M Smucker (SJM). McCormick shares are up 15% YTD versus a 23% gain in the S&P 500.
